      • hitTest(r: RenderDecoratedBox; htResult: HitTestResult; position: Offset): bool

        performLayout
      • performLayout(r: RenderDecoratedBox)

BoxDecoration and RenderDecoratedBox: background fill, border, shadow, border radius, and shape. This is what gives Container and Card their visual style.

Imports

../foundation/render_object, ../foundation/geometry, ../foundation/color

Types

Border = object
  color*: Color
  width*: float32
A simple uniform border. Set width = 0 for no border. 
BoxDecoration = object
  color*: Color
  gradient*: Gradient
  border*: Border
  borderRadius*: float32
  shape*: BoxShape
  shadows*: seq[BoxShadow]
  hasGradient*: bool
  hasBorder*: bool

Visual style of a decorated box. Build via boxDecoration(...).

Fields:

  • color: solid fill color.
  • gradient: NOT YET RENDERED.
  • border: uniform-width outline.
  • borderRadius: corner rounding in logical pixels.
  • shape: rectangle or circle.
  • shadows: list of drop shadows. Rendered as solid offset rectangles (no blur yet).
  • hasGradient, hasBorder: opt-in flags so default zero values don't accidentally enable rendering.
BoxShadow = object
  color*: Color
  offset*: Offset
  blur*: float32
  spread*: float32
A drop shadow attached to a BoxDecoration. Currently rendered as a solid-colored rect at offset inflated by spread; the blur field is stored but not yet rasterized (the canvas backends lack proper gaussian blur). 
BoxShape = enum
  bsRectangle, bsCircle
Outline shape of a BoxDecoration. bsRectangle (the default) fills with borderRadius rounding; bsCircle fills a circle whose diameter is min(box.width, box.height). 
Gradient = object
  kind*: GradientKind
  begin*: Alignment
  colors*: seq[Color]
  stops*: seq[float32]
Linear or radial gradient definition. Currently a placeholder: BoxDecoration.gradient is stored but not drawn. Reserved API surface for forward compatibility. 
GradientKind = enum
  gkLinear, gkRadial
Gradient flavor. gkLinear interpolates along a line from begin to end alignment; gkRadial interpolates outward from begin. Neither is rendered today; flit's canvas backends don't yet implement gradient fills. 
RenderDecoratedBox = ref object of RenderObject
  decoration*: BoxDecoration
  child*: RenderObject
Render object that paints a BoxDecoration underneath its optional child. Backs the DecoratedBox widget.

Procs

proc boxDecoration(color = colorTransparent; borderRadius = 0.0'f32;
                   border = Border(color: colorTransparent, width: 0.0);
                   shape = bsRectangle): BoxDecoration {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

Builds a BoxDecoration from the most common knobs.

Inputs:

  • color: solid fill color. Default colorTransparent (no fill).
  • borderRadius: corner rounding in logical pixels. 0 = sharp corners.
  • border: a Border value. Set border.width > 0 to enable an outline. Default no border.
  • shape: bsRectangle (default) or bsCircle.

Output: a BoxDecoration value ready to pass to decoratedBox(...) or to assign to Container.decoration.

Methods

method hitTest(r: RenderDecoratedBox; htResult: HitTestResult; position: Offset): bool {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Forwards to the child if any, then adds itself so a wrapping GestureDetector is reachable. Decorations themselves don't consume taps; the underlying rectangle does. 
method paint(r: RenderDecoratedBox; ctx: PaintingContext; offset: Offset) {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}

Paints the decoration in this order: shadows (back-most), then the fill shape (rectangle / rounded rect / circle), then the border outline (currently four line segments), then the child on top.

Note: shadows are drawn as solid offset rects (no gaussian blur), gradients are not drawn at all. See BoxDecoration's type doc for the limitations.

method performLayout(r: RenderDecoratedBox) {....raises: [Exception],
    tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Sizes the box: with a child, passes parent constraints through and adopts the child's size. With no child, fills bounded constraints (so a standalone decoration is visible). Decoration painting itself is parameterless w.r.t. layout - it just fills whatever size we end up with.
